commit.name: Multi-Step Survey Form
commit.description: Multi-step form with validation, progress tracking, and review
commit.prompt:
Build a multi-step survey form with 4 steps:
- Step 1: Text inputs for name (required) and email (required, must be valid email format) with inline validation
- Step 2: Radio button group for satisfaction rating (1-5 scale with labels from props)
- Step 3: Checkbox group for feature interests (options from props)
- Step 4: Textarea for open-ended comments (optional, with character counter, max 500 chars)

Requirements:
- Progress bar at the top showing current step (e.g., "Step 2 of 4")
- Back/Next navigation buttons (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Final review step showing all answers before submission
- Call props.onSubmit with all collected answers as a structured object
- All option labels and feature lists must come from props
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

featureOptions.description: Available feature options for the checkbox step

satisfactionLabels.description: Labels for satisfaction rating (e.g., Very Unsatisfied to Very Satisfied)

submit.label: Submit Survey
submit.description: Called with all collected answers as a structured object

evaluation.critique: The component demonstrates solid implementation of a multi-step form with proper layout using Stack/Card patterns and good use of ggui design tokens (spacing, colors). Minor issues include incomplete Step 3 code (cut off mid-implementation), missing visual feedback for button states during form submission, and no clear disabled state styling for the Next button when validation fails.

commit.name: Kanban Task Board
commit.description: Column-based task board with task cards and state management
commit.prompt:
Build a Kanban-style task board with 3 columns: To Do, In Progress, Done.

Each column:
- Has a header with column name and task count
- Contains a vertical list of task cards
- Has an "Add Task" button at the bottom

Each task card displays:
- Task title (editable on double-click or via edit button)
- Priority badge (low/medium/high with color coding from design tokens)
- Assignee avatar or initials
- Due date (if set)
- A move button or dropdown to move the task to another column

Requirements:
- Tasks can be moved between columns via move controls (buttons or select dropdown)
- New tasks start in "To Do" column
- "Add Task" shows an inline form with title input and priority selector
- Track total tasks and completed count in a summary bar at the top
- Initial tasks come from props; real-time updates from other team members arrive via stream
- Call the taskUpdate action when a task is moved, edited, or created
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors, spacing, and shadows

tasks.description: Array of tasks with id, title, priority (low/medium/high), assignee initials, column ID, and optional dueDate

columns.description: Column definitions: [{id: "todo", name: "To Do"}, {id: "in-progress", name: "In Progress"}, {id: "done", name: "Done"}]

taskUpdate.label: Task Updated
taskUpdate.description: Called when a task is moved, edited, or created. Payload: { action, taskId, data }

stream.description: Real-time task updates from other team members

taskChanged.description: A task was moved, edited, or created by another user — update it in the board

getBoardState.description: Current board state including any unsaved edits

evaluation.critique: The component demonstrates strong design token usage and proper data handling, with a clear multi-step flow and review screen. However, the layout has minor spacing inconsistencies (step 5 cuts off mid-review), and Polish is reduced due to missing visual feedback states like loading indicators during submission and inconsistent focus/hover styling on buttons. The component would benefit from smoother transitions between steps and clearer disabled state styling.

commit.name: Kanban Task Board
commit.description: Column-based task board with task cards and state management
commit.prompt:
Build a Kanban-style task board with 3 columns: To Do, In Progress, Done.

Each column:
- Has a header with column name and task count
- Contains a vertical list of task cards
- Has an "Add Task" button at the bottom

Each task card displays:
- Task title (editable on double-click or via edit button)
- Priority badge (low/medium/high with color coding from design tokens)
- Assignee avatar or initials
- Due date (if set)
- A move button or dropdown to move the task to another column

Requirements:
- Tasks can be moved between columns via move controls (buttons or select dropdown)
- New tasks start in "To Do" column
- "Add Task" shows an inline form with title input and priority selector
- Track total tasks and completed count in a summary bar at the top
- Initial tasks come from props; real-time updates from other team members arrive via stream
- Call the taskUpdate action when a task is moved, edited, or created
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors, spacing, and shadows

tasks.description: Array of tasks with id, title, priority (low/medium/high), assignee initials, column ID, and optional dueDate

columns.description: Column definitions: [{id: "todo", name: "To Do"}, {id: "in-progress", name: "In Progress"}, {id: "done", name: "Done"}]

taskUpdate.label: Task Updated
taskUpdate.description: Called when a task is moved, edited, or created. Payload: { action, taskId, data }

stream.description: Real-time task updates from other team members

taskChanged.description: A task was moved, edited, or created by another user — update it in the board

getBoardState.description: Current board state including any unsaved edits

issues.description: New task creation ignores the target column: `createTask(columnId)` always sets `column: 'todo'` and sends `taskUpdate` with `column: 'todo'`, so creating from other columns does not place tasks in that column as expected from the per-column Add Task flow. Use `columnId` for the created task's column and action payload.
issues.fix: New task creation ignores the target column: `createTask(columnId)` always sets `column: 'todo'` and sends `taskUpdate` with `column: 'todo'`, so creating from other columns does not place tasks in that column as expected from the per-column Add Task flow. Use `columnId` for the created task's column and action payload.

issues.description: Real-time delete handling is broken for stream events without `task.id`: the code generates a new fallback id (`stream-${Date.now()}`) and filters by it, so the intended task is not removed. Handle delete events only when a valid task id is provided (or use a stable identifier from the event).
issues.fix: Real-time delete handling is broken for stream events without `task.id`: the code generates a new fallback id (`stream-${Date.now()}`) and filters by it, so the intended task is not removed. Handle delete events only when a valid task id is provided (or use a stable identifier from the event).

issues.description: Task cards are only navigable via per-card controls; there is no list-level keyboard navigation/selection mechanism (e.g., roving focus or selectable card container), making dense task lists harder to navigate efficiently. Add keyboard-navigable card focus/selection for the task list.
issues.fix: Task cards are only navigable via per-card controls; there is no list-level keyboard navigation/selection mechanism (e.g., roving focus or selectable card container), making dense task lists harder to navigate efficiently. Add keyboard-navigable card focus/selection for the task list.

issues.description: Task title inline edit trigger relies on double-clicking a Text rendered as span (line 215–223) with no explicit keyboard interaction (e.g., Enter/Space) on that element. Add a keyboard-accessible trigger (button or key handlers with proper role/tabIndex) so keyboard-only users can initiate edit from the title control.
issues.fix: Task title inline edit trigger relies on double-clicking a Text rendered as span (line 215–223) with no explicit keyboard interaction (e.g., Enter/Space) on that element. Add a keyboard-accessible trigger (button or key handlers with proper role/tabIndex) so keyboard-only users can initiate edit from the title control.

issues.description: Task update/create/move actions have no failure handling path: `taskUpdate(...)` is called without `try/catch` or action error-state subscription, so operation failures cannot surface `updateError`. Add async error handling and set an error message when the action fails.
issues.fix: Task update/create/move actions have no failure handling path: `taskUpdate(...)` is called without `try/catch` or action error-state subscription, so operation failures cannot surface `updateError`. Add async error handling and set an error message when the action fails.

evaluation.critique: The component has decent data handling and structure but suffers from incomplete implementation (code is cut off), missing design token usage throughout (hardcoded colors implied in badge mapping), and lack of visual polish like transitions and interactive states. The layout foundation is present but needs responsive refinement and proper spacing using design tokens.

Use `columnId` for the created task's column and action payload.
